Reasoning: Dizziness can result from electrolyte imbalances (e.g., hyponatremia) or severe anemia causing cerebral hypoxia.
Light-headedness and near-syncope are clinical signs of orthostatic hypotension often linked to dehydration or anemia.
Blurred vision is a primary symptom of hyperglycemia in Diabetes Mellitus due to osmotic changes in the lens.
Memory and concentration problems (confusion) are signs of metabolic encephalopathy from renal or liver failure.
Depressive symptoms are associated with various chronic medical conditions and nutritional deficiencies like Vitamin D.
Nocturia is a common symptom of Diabetes Mellitus (osmotic diuresis) and Chronic Kidney Disease.
Fatigue is a hallmark of Anemia, Chronic Kidney Disease, and Hepatic Failure.
Appetite changes are key diagnostic criteria for mood disorders and symptoms of metabolic/endocrine dysfunction.
Difficulty concentrating is a common manifestation of depressive disorders and nutritional deficiencies (e.g., Vitamin B12).
